name: MemeOps
tagline: A static API for operational truth, architectural regret, and enterprise-grade nonsense.

categories.title: Architecture
categories.summary: Judgment lines for expensive technical posture, decorative scale, and self-inflicted distributed systems.
notes: These entries roast complexity, not ambition.
Use them when a diagram has more moving parts than active users.

categories.title: Incident
categories.summary: Operationally polished phrases for outages, near misses, and the art of saying the system was fine until users touched it.
notes: These lines should sound plausible in a tired comms channel.
Credibility reflects how long the sentence buys you before follow-up questions.

categories.title: Agent
categories.summary: Performance reviews for coding agents, prompt workers, and synthetic coworkers who move faster than their consequences.
notes: These reviews should sound calm while describing real operational risk.
Reliability is a score, not an act of faith.

categories.title: Decision
categories.summary: Opinionated answers for recurring engineering choices that usually get over-discussed and under-bounded.
notes: The useful answer is usually the one with less ceremony.
Confidence should rise when the boring option is obviously correct.

categories.title: Postmortem
categories.summary: Mock incident narratives that read like real operational pain with better grammar and fewer redactions.
notes: These entries should feel like incidents you could plausibly have suffered through.
Keep the lesson sharp enough to outlive the meeting.

notes: The point is translation, not diplomacy.

categories.title: Token
categories.summary: Judgment for wasteful AI usage, budget theater, and prompt loops that should have been one comment.
notes: Token guilt works best when the spend and outcome are both painfully legible.
Finance language should remain just formal enough to sting.

categories.title: Startup
categories.summary: Absurd but plausible company ideas for an economy still trying to expense its way into product truth.
notes: The joke lands when the buyer and pain are almost credible.
Moats should sound fundable and operationally suspect.

categories.title: Observability
categories.summary: Signals, dashboards, and telemetry patterns that look useful right up until someone asks for an answer.
notes: These entries describe instrumentation posture more than instrumentation coverage.
The next move should create clarity, not more charts.

categories.title: Deploy
categories.summary: Release patterns, rollback anxiety, and deployment language that sounds routine until the hidden dependency logs in.
notes: These entries should read like a release note written by someone already opening rollback tabs.
Containment is what kept the incident from gaining a name.

categories.title: Runbook
categories.summary: Operational procedure fragments for incidents where the official playbook contains equal parts wisdom and superstition.
notes: The best runbooks distinguish signal from ritual.
Exit conditions matter more than number of steps.

categories.summary: Response-time narratives for systems that stayed alive just long enough to ruin trust instead of availability charts.
notes: These entries should make room for both dashboard shape and customer pain.
Mitigation should sound more useful than the average war-room suggestion.

categories.summary: Pages, moods, coping patterns, and the thin layer of professionalism separating investigation from folklore.
notes: Operator mood belongs in the format because everyone can already see it.
Scar tissue is accumulated system knowledge with worse branding.

categories.summary: Pull request and design review lines where the visible comment and the actual concern are politely decoupled.
notes: The comment text and the subtext should both be useful.
Mergeability is social truth wearing process language.

categories.title: Compliance
categories.summary: Formal control language for environments where the spreadsheet, the screenshot, and the human memory are still technically part of the system.
notes: Keep the control posture polished and the plain truth sharper than it wants to be.
Compensating control is where operational character becomes visible.

categories.title: Roadmap
categories.summary: Promises, dependencies, and strategic hedges for plans that are one staffing change away from becoming philosophy.
notes: A roadmap item is a social contract with poor error handling.
The hedge should sound familiar to anyone who has shipped through reorg season.

categories.summary: Recurring calendar rituals for alignment, deniability, and the steady conversion of ambiguity into notes no one will reopen.
